channel.title: +Vaccination on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Vaccination on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Approaches
item.description: mRNA for spike protein COVID-19 mRNA vaccines give instructions for our cells to make “spike protein”, which gets desposited on cell surface. Then, an immune response to them devolops. Spike protein injection Trigger antibody development thence. Foiling Spike protein Idea is to bind and block S proteins. ACE2 decoys Entry of the virus which happens through binding of Spike (S) protein of the virus with ACE2 receptors found in the lungs.

item.title: Herd immunity levels
item.description: With the original Wuhan virus,experts felt once 67% of the population are protected cases will come down. Next came Alpha variant( B 117) this number got increased to 75% Then came Delta variant( B 1.617.2) this further increased to 83%.

item.title: India
item.description: Approvals J&J vaccine will ‘get back on track soon’ Sputnik V 202104 Bharat Biotech - approved “in public interest as an abundant precaution, in clinical trial mode, to have more options for vaccinations, especially in case of infection by mutant strains”. Oxford/ AstraZeneca. manufactured locally by the Serum Institute of India. Immunization priority 60% of vaccines globally, plans to immunise about 300 million people by July 2021. It will prioritise health care workers, the emergency services, and those who are clinically vulnerable because of age or pre-existing conditions.

item.title: Other Trials as of 2020-12
item.description: FDA standards - “If a particular vaccine doesn’t demonstrate a minimum of 50 percent efficacy, it will not be approved.”

item.title: Side effect mitigation
item.description: Thrombosis with Thrombocytopenia Syndrome (TTS) Reference sheet here.

item.title: Vipin's summary
item.description: 1-Ten different Covid-19 vaccines have shared their Phase III data; 2 based on mRNA, 4 on viral vector, 3 on inactivated & 1 on protein subunit platform 2-After 4 months of their trials, now there is a definite gradient in their performance: Highly efficacious: mRNA, moderate: viral vector, & low to moderate: inactivated vaccines. 3- Efficacy (Point estimates as per published/shared data): Pfizer 95%; Moderna 94.1%, Sputnik-V 91.6%, Novavax 89.
